Title: Genome-Wide Identification and Characterization of NAC Family in Hibiscus hamabo Sieb. et Zucc. under Various Abiotic Stresses

Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. HhNAC54 is a member of the NAC transcription factor family identified from the Hibiscus hamabo genome.
2. Transcriptome analysis and qRT-PCR show that HhNAC54 is strongly induced by drought (PEG), salt (NaCl), ABA, and MeJA treatments, indicating a role in multiple abiotic stress responses.
3. Overexpression of HhNAC54 in Arabidopsis thaliana (Col-0) results in transgenic lines whose root growth is less inhibited by high salinity than wild type on NaCl-containing medium, demonstrating that HhNAC54 positively contributes to salt tolerance.
4. HhNAC54 is thus a key stress-responsive NAC gene from H. hamabo and a promising candidate for dissecting abiotic stress response mechanisms and improving salt tolerance in plants.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of H. hamabo roots under drought and salt stress (FPKM-based expression profiles of all 182 HhNAC genes, including HhNAC54; accession not specified).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of HhNAC54 enhances salt tolerance, as shown by significantly less root growth inhibition and longer roots of transgenic Arabidopsis compared with wild type under high NaCl conditions.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor family with conserved N-terminal NAC domain
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Strongly upregulated under drought (PEG), salt (NaCl), ABA, and MeJA treatments in H. hamabo, with qRT-PCR expression trends consistent with RNA-seq data, indicating induction at multiple time points during stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and salt tolerance regulation
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (salt stress, drought/osmotic stress, ABA and MeJA response)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under NaCl stress, roots of HhNAC54-overexpressing Arabidopsis lines were significantly longer than those of wild type (p<0.01), indicating reduced inhibition of root growth by high salinity.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: H. hamabo seedlings (8–10 true leaves) treated with 400 mM NaCl, 15% PEG 6000, 50 μM ABA, and 1 mM MeJA; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 plants transformed with pCAMBIA1305-HhNAC54, hygromycin-selected homozygous T3 overexpression lines (OE1–OE3) and wild-type controls.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of NAC genes from the H. hamabo genome; RNA-seq of roots under drought (PEG) and salt (NaCl) stress; qRT-PCR validation of selected HhNAC genes including HhNAC54 under PEG, NaCl, ABA and MeJA treatments; generation of Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 transgenic lines overexpressing HhNAC54; salt tolerance assays on 1/2 MS medium with different NaCl concentrations; root length measurements and statistical analysis using Student's t-test.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by overexpressing HhNAC54 in Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 (homozygous T3 lines OE1–OE3) followed by salt tolerance assays on NaCl-supplemented 1/2 MS medium and comparison of root lengths between transgenic and wild-type plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from genome-wide NAC family screening of the Hibiscus hamabo genome using HMM profiles of NAM and NAC domains; selected for functional analysis based on transcriptome and qRT-PCR expression patterns under abiotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: HhNAC54 is a stress-inducible NAC transcription factor from the salt- and drought-tolerant semi-mangrove tree Hibiscus hamabo that enhances salt tolerance when overexpressed. It provides an excellent gene resource for molecular breeding and genetic engineering of salt-tolerant woody and semi-mangrove plants and can be used as a candidate marker or transgene to improve salt tolerance in other crops.
